topic.what: cross-layer authentication scheme integrating ECC-based PKI with PHY-layer hardware fingerprinting and ML re-authentication for healthcare information exchange networks
topic.how: combines initial ECC-PKI handshake with lightweight PHY-layer re-authentication using CFO and quadrature skewness features extracted from OFDM symbols, classified by ML (KNN) for continuous device verification
topic.why_matters: solves security-efficiency tradeoff in resource-constrained HIE environments where traditional cryptographic authentication incurs prohibitive overhead for real-time healthcare communications

characteristics.what_it_measures: ML-based device identification accuracy across varying network densities
characteristics.vs_baseline: compares KNN vs logistic regression, neural networks, decision trees, gradient boosted trees
characteristics.evidence: Table V reports KNN achieving 89.85% (20 devices) to 78.75% (30 devices)
characteristics.context: KNN outperforms alternatives especially at higher device densities (30 devices)

characteristics.vs_baseline: vs 8.979-44.676ms for Qi-Xie, Xiang, Kumar, Chen schemes
characteristics.context: re-authentication uses only CFO extraction and KNN classification, no ECC operations

characteristics.vs_baseline: vs 240n-984n bytes for competing schemes at n=1000
characteristics.evidence: Fig 8 shows 6.156KB vs 61.5KB-984KB for others at 1000 messages
characteristics.context: initial auth 144 bytes; re-auth only sends PID (20 bytes)

characteristics.what_it_measures: formal security verification using BAN logic
characteristics.vs_baseline: vs informal or heuristic analysis in competing works
characteristics.evidence: Section V-A: Goals 1-7 including mutual auth, shared key establishment, replay resistance
characteristics.context: proves resilience against impersonation, MitM, replay, Sybil attacks

characteristics.what_itasures: maximum number of simultaneously authenticated devices
characteristics.evidence: Table V: accuracy drops from ~89% (20 devices) to ~78% (30 devices)
characteristics.context: KNN maintains best performance at high density among evaluated ML algorithms
KNN achieves lowest FPR at 30 devices
